text: Capstone (proposed): proof-decomposition granularity is a controllable training-data axis for small theorem provers - varying rough (pi_root) vs fine (pi_leaf) decomposition of the SAME verified proofs, unlike prior work that varies decomposition only at inference time. Established: the section formalism (a-0001), a machine-VERIFIED 607-pair reconstruction pipeline (a-0002), the data-sufficiency signal (a-0003, marginal), the binary recalibration (a-0004), and a clean 0/50 pre-SFT floor (a-0005). Unproven: the headline empirical claim that granularity changes what small provers learn, growing as they shrink (a-0006), blocked on Modal SFT.
